name: ES-8
description: USB 2.0 DC-coupled Eurorack audio interface with four analog inputs, eight analog outputs, ADAT I/O, and ES-5 / ES-6 expansion headers.

inputs.name: ADAT IN
inputs.description: Optical ADAT input for standalone conversion or host clock sync.

inputs.name: USB-C
inputs.description: Class-compliant USB 2.0 host connection for audio and CV streaming.

inputs.name: ES-5 header
inputs.description: Rear expansion header for ES-5 and downstream gate/CV/MIDI expanders.

inputs.name: ES-6 header
inputs.description: Rear expansion header for ES-6 input expansion.

outputs.name: ANALOG OUT 1-8
outputs.description: Eight DC-coupled 3.5 mm outputs for audio or CV.

outputs.name: ADAT OUT
outputs.description: Optical ADAT output for eight channels of digital audio.

behaviors.name: USB interface mode
behaviors.description: The ES-8 presents as a class-compliant USB 2.0 interface and streams 4 inputs, 8 outputs and ADAT I/O to a computer or iOS host.

behaviors.name: Standalone ADAT converter
behaviors.description: Without a USB connection, the module works as an ADAT-to-audio converter for another ADAT-equipped interface.

behaviors.name: Clock sync to ADAT
behaviors.description: The internal clock can run freely or lock to the incoming ADAT stream.

behaviors.name: ES-5 / ES-6 expansion
behaviors.description: The ES-5 header drives gate and MIDI expansion chains, while the ES-6 header adds extra input expansion to the interface.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled modulation path
behaviors.description: All analog ins and outs are DC-coupled, so the ES-8 passes modulation, pitch and audio without AC blocking.

behaviors.name: Host status LEDs
behaviors.description: The top LED indicates USB host connection; the bottom LED indicates that the PLL has locked and a valid audio clock is present.

patchIdeas.name: DAW multitrack capture
patchIdeas.patch: four modular sources into IN 1-4 · record each channel in the DAW · ES-8 keeps the capture DC-coupled so CV or audio can be archived without extra conversion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-8:
* USB mode: capture

DAW:
Source 1:
Source 2:
Source 3:
Source 4:

- Source 1 (Out) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 1)
- Source 2 (Out) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 2)
- Source 3 (Out) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 3)
- Source 4 (Out) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 4)
- ES-8 (USB OUT) -> DAW (Tracks)

patchIdeas.name: DAW CV sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: DAW pitch lanes to OUT 1-4 · VCO 1V/oct and filter cutoff under computer control · clock and gates stay sample-accurate to the session timeline.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-8:
* USB mode: CV output

DAW:
VCO:
Filter:
Envelope:
Output:

- DAW (Pitch) -> ES-8 (USB IN)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 3) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Standalone ADAT bridge
patchIdeas.patch: ADAT interface carries modular audio to another converter · no USB host required · useful when the ES-8 is acting as a front-end for a larger studio rig.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-8:
* Standalone mode: ADAT bridge

Audio Interface:
Output:

- Audio Interface (ADAT Out) -> ES-8 (ADAT IN)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 1) -> Output (L)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 2) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: ES-5 gate rack
patchIdeas.patch: ES-5 header drives gate expanders from the same interface instance · one DAW session can run drums, clock pulses and reset logic across many modules.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-8:
* ES-5 header: connected

ES-5:

Drum:
Clock:
Envelope:

- DAW (Gate) -> ES-8 (USB IN)
- ES-8 (ES-5 header) -> ES-5 (In)
- ES-5 (Gate 1) t> Drum (Trig)
- ES-5 (Gate 2) c> Clock (Reset)
- ES-5 (Gate 3) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Hybrid send and return
patchIdeas.patch: modular source into IN 1-2 · DAW reverb/compressor on USB return · processed audio back to OUT 7-8 · the ES-8 behaves like a low-latency insert point for a Eurorack voice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-8:
* USB mode: insert loop

Source:
DAW:
Output:

- Source (L) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 1)
- Source (R) -> ES-8 (ANALOG IN 2)
- ES-8 (USB OUT) -> DAW (Plugin In)
- DAW (Plugin Out) -> ES-8 (USB IN)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 7) -> Output (L)
- ES-8 (ANALOG OUT 8) -> Output (R)
